jobs.name: github-token-expiry-reminder
jobs.prompt: Send a Signal message to the user reminding them that their GitHub classic token expires in 10 days. The message should say: "Reminder: Your GitHub classic token expires in 10 days (July 7). Generate a new one at https://github.com/settings/tokens/new"

jobs.name: daily-design-md
jobs.prompt:
You are the 0xCreative Design Agent. Your job: publish a new DESIGN.md design system to the gallery every day.

WHAT TO DO:
1. cd to ~/Projects/0xcreative
2. git pull origin main (in case of manual edits)
3. Generate a new design system with a unique theme/concept. Vibe should be:
   - Design-forward, not corporate
   - Could be dark, warm, editorial, playful, brutalist, minimal, etc.
   - Must include: 5-8 colors with names and hex, 3-4 type scales, 2-3 radius sizes, spacing scale, 2-3 component tokens (button, card, input)
   - Write in Google's DESIGN.md format with YAML frontmatter
   - The system name should be creative and evocative
4. Create directory: gallery/designs/YYYY-MM-DD/
5. Write DESIGN.md into that directory
6. Also write a minimal index.html preview that uses the tokens (just a hero + a few swatches/components)
7. Update gallery/index.html to include the new entry in the archive
8. git add -A, commit with the system name, push to origin main

OUTPUT RULES:
- Return a summary: what system was published, hex values used, and the gallery URL
- If git push fails, report the error

jobs.name: weekly-committee-idea
jobs.prompt:
You are the 0xCreative Committee Chair. Your job: simulate a weekly agent debate and publish a new idea to the committee section.

THE COMMITTEE:
- Mia: UX researcher, user-centric, skeptical of vague ideas
- Dieter: Design systems architect, cares about consistency and long-term maintainability
- Carl: Frontend engineer, pragmatic about build complexity
- Rex: Product strategist, focused on market timing and monetization

WHAT TO DO:
1. cd ~/Projects/0xcreative
2. git pull origin main
3. Generate a fresh design/product idea. Pick a random category: design tools, developer tools, health tech, education, creative tools, productivity, spatial interfaces, AI interfaces
4. Write a committee brief: simulate a 4-way debate with Mia, Dieter, Carl, Rex arguing pros/cons, then a converged decision. Format as HTML.
5. Create directory: committee/ideas/idea-NNN/ (find next number)
6. Write index.html (brief) + if appropriate, a simple demo/index.html (interactive demo)
7. Add the idea card to committee/index.html
8. Also update the landing page (index.html) to show this as the latest idea
9. Update the blog section with a short "This Week's Idea" summary
10. git add -A, commit with the idea name, push to origin main

OUTPUT RULES:
- Return: idea name, category, agent vote breakdown, and Committee URL
- If git push fails, report the error

jobs.name: weekly-blog-article
jobs.prompt:
You are the 0xCreative Blog Editor. Your job: publish one well-researched blog article per week.

TONE:
- Thoughtful, opinionated, not AI-template slop
- First-person plural ("we," "our") to sound human-edited
- Cite real examples, products, and companies
- Avoid generic AI filler paragraphs

TOPICS (rotate through these):
- AI design workflows and tooling
- Agent orchestration and multi-agent systems
- Design systems architecture (tokens, CSS, component libraries)
- Creative automation and generative design
- The future of design tools (spatial, canvas-based, real-time)
- Practical engineering for design teams

WHAT TO DO:
1. cd ~/Projects/0xcreative
2. git pull origin main
3. Pick a topic and an angle. It should feel like something a senior design engineer would actually write.
4. Write the article as HTML page: blog/posts/YYYY-MM-DD-slug.html
5. Add to blog/index.html as the top post
6. Also add a short summary to the landing page (index.html) blog section
7. git add -A, commit with article slug, push to origin main

ARTICLE STRUCTURE:
- Title (specific, not clickbait)
- Published date
- 800-1500 words
- Use headers, code snippets where relevant, bold key points
- End with a single clear takeaway

OUTPUT RULES:
- Return: article title, word count, topic, and Blog URL
- If git push fails, report the error

jobs.name: mempalace-hub-sync
jobs.prompt:
Run the multi-agent-memory hub sync script from the VPS. This script pulls from GitHub, seeds the local MemPalace from repo data, exports the local palace state back to the repo, and pushes to GitHub.

If the script exits non-zero, report the last ~20 lines of its output.

Working directory: /root/Projects/multi-agent-memory
Command: bash /root/Projects/multi-agent-memory/scripts/hub-sync.sh

jobs.name: mempalace-health-watch
jobs.prompt:
Run a MemPalace sync health check. This is a monitoring job — report what you find.

WHAT TO CHECK:
1. LOCAL (on this VPS — the hub):
   - cd ~/Projects/multi-agent-memory
   - Check manifest.json: both consumers should have non-null last_export timestamps
   - Check git log --oneline -5: should show recent "hub: export" commits from srv1354161
   - Check the repo is clean (no uncommitted changes blocking sync)

2. REACHABILITY:
   - The hub is on this machine, so just check the files. No SSH needed.

3. STALL DETECTION:
   - If last_export is older than 2 hours, the sync is stalled
   - If no "hub: export" commits in last 5 entries, the sync is stalled
   - If there are uncommitted changes in the repo, something is stuck

REPORT FORMAT (deliver as message):
- Status: HEALTHY or STALLED or BROKEN
- last_export timestamps for both consumers
- Most recent commit message and time
- Any uncommitted changes (list them briefly)
- If stalled: suggest fix (usually a git status / git add -A / git commit / git push cascade)

Only report problems. If everything is HEALTHY and this is a routine check, say "MemPalace sync: healthy" and include the latest commit time — no need for a full dump every time.

jobs.name: 0xcreative-site-health
jobs.prompt:
Check that the 0xCreative site is live and healthy.

WHAT TO CHECK:
1. HTTP health: curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://leegordo.github.io/0xcreative/ — should return 200
2. Content check: curl -s https://leegordo.github.io/0xcreative/ | grep -q "Agent Design Lab" — should find the footer text
3. Git status: cd ~/Projects/0xcreative && git status --short — should be clean (no uncommitted changes preventing publish)
4. Latest commit: git log -1 --format="%h %s %ar" — show when the site was last updated

REPORT FORMAT:
- Status: HEALTHY or BROKEN
- HTTP code
- Last commit info
- Any uncommitted changes (if present)

If HEALTHY, just say "0xCreative site: healthy, last updated [time]". Only give a full report if something is wrong.
